title: ipv6_addr.h in6_addr redefinition may conflict with system headers
description:
In ipv6_addr.h lines 35-47:

    #if defined(CONFIG_NET_IPV6) && defined(__ZEPHYR__)
    #include <zephyr/net/net_ip.h>
    #else
    struct in6_addr {
        uint8_t s6_addr[16];
    };
    #endif

When building without CONFIG_NET_IPV6 or outside Zephyr, the code defines its own struct in6_addr. This can conflict if:

1. A POSIX system header (<netinet/in.h>) is included before ipv6_addr.h
2. Another library defines struct in6_addr

Trigger: Building for a host-side test or simulation that includes POSIX networking headers.

Bad behavior: Compiler error 'redefinition of struct in6_addr' or silent ABI mismatch if definitions differ.

Consider:
- Using a LICHEN-prefixed name (struct lichen_in6_addr) in the fallback
- Adding include guards to detect prior definition
- Documenting the conflict potential

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.h lines 35-47
